title: Peppery Mexican Onion Crisp
description: A unique halal-friendly solo-dinner dish featuring caramelized onion rings seasoned with a spicy peppery blend for an unexpected twist on traditional Mexican cuisine.
instructions: Slice the onion into rings, about 1/8 inch thick.
In a large skillet over medium heat, add oil and chopped peppers. Cook until fragrant and slightly softened, about 2 minutes.
Add minced garlic and cumin seeds to the skillet; cook for another minute.
Toss the onion rings into the skillet with the peppers and garlic. Add salt and mix well.
Reduce heat to low and cover the skillet to allow the onions to sweat until slightly translucent, about 10-15 minutes.
Increase heat to medium-high and add a bit more oil if needed. Cook the onion slices on both sides for another 5 minutes or until they start caramelizing but are still crisp.
Turn off the heat and let the onion rings sit in their own juices, slightly cooled down before serving.
